pattern: Map each uppercase ASCII letter to its lowercase counterpart by shifting its code point, leaving every other character untouched.
hints: Only the 26 uppercase letters change; digits, spaces, and symbols stay exactly as they are.
An uppercase letter and its lowercase form differ by a constant gap in their character codes.
Walk the string, and for any character in the A to Z range add 32 to its code point; concatenate the results.

edge_inputs.description: digits and symbols must be preserved
characters near the letter range boundaries

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(chr(rng.randrange(32, 127)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: strings heavy on letters across both cases
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    pool = string.ascii_letters
    s = ''.join(pool[rng.randrange(len(pool))] for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
